title: BTC futures strategy (2019–2025) – feedback on fees, execution & exchange choice
author: Economy_Fun5953
selftext:
Hi all,  
Looking for **technical feedback** from traders who’ve run systematic strategies for multiple cycles.

**Strategy overview:**

* BTC futures
* Tested across **2019–2025**
* \~3 years live trading
* Fully rule-based, now automated due to missed trades during sleep hours
* Execution: **100% market orders**
* Fees modeled at **0.3% per trade** (Binance fees + slippage + funding)

All results, trades, and equity curves are attached as images.

Things I’d like feedback on:

* Do the returns and drawdowns look reasonable across different regimes (bull, bear, chop)?
* Is 0.3% per trade sufficient, or should I model higher during high volatility?
* Market vs limit orders for automation — worth switching or not?
* Exchange choice for automation: Binance vs **Bitget / Blofin**
* Anyone running **TradingView webhooks directly to exchange APIs** without third-party services?

Not promoting anything — genuinely trying to stress-test assumptions before scaling risk.

comments.author: disaster_story_69
comments.body: The max drawdown would (for me) make this unfeasible. what do the sharpe scores look like? Also the very low volume of trades would mean long waits between trades.

replies.author: Economy_Fun5953
replies.body:
Yeah the max drawdown is after 9x leverage but i am pretty much comfortable with it. As i have given kill switch command like if my trade have 4 consecutive losses then stop trading. Its per my data i have maximum 5 trade losses in a row.

I have uploaded the all the analysis details in the picture you need to slide left for other pictures. But yes the sharp ratio is 0.331. 

Lmk if you know anything on my other questions like i am planning to change exchange to bitget or blofin which has direct integration like binance webhook signal.

replies.author: disaster_story_69
replies.body: Ah ok, so that’s reasonable. Sharpe ratio v low though

replies.author: Economy_Fun5953
replies.body:
Thanks mannn! Plz lmk if you got any ideas on good exchanges with webhook signal.

Also for drawdown i have taken aggressive fees like 0.3% per trade. But in real its close to 0.15% per trade. 

I use btc/usdc pair on binance with bnb fee deduction option. And all my trades execute in market order. Hence now i am planning to test limit order side by side.

Also, i am comfortable with less trades until i having good profits. So its all happened because i continuously traded and experimented this strategy for 6 years now.

replies.author: disaster_story_69
replies.body: Good luck, no interest in pivotting to forex where there is far less volatility, infinite liquidity and access to leverage? I use fusion markets for forex, tighest spreads available

replies.author: Economy_Fun5953
replies.body:
Honestly, i never tried exploring forex or other markets. Its just recently i started this that to in few trading competitions where i only need to trade gold, silver or forex. I use just normal TA on these markets the basic ones. And i feel since i am into this field for 6 years now, so i can trade anything.

In fact i just use 1 assest (btc) 1 time frame and and 2 -3 strategy to test it out and i believe that why i am profitable alhamdullilah. 

I tell u story when i was getting married and did TA on gold for longer period and when i saw a chance to grab the opportunity the same i went to market to buy all the necessary gold jewellery. I posted this on my LinkedIn.
